name: The Eagle
description: Historic pub where Watson and Crick discussed the structure of DNA. Cosy panelled rooms and a famous RAF bar.

name: The Mill
description: Riverside pub by the Cam with a large terrace. Popular with students and punters.

name: The Pickerel Inn
description: One of Cambridge's oldest pubs. Cosy fireside seating and a relaxed atmosphere.

name: Fort St George
description: Riverside pub on Midsummer Common with great views of the Cam and punting.

name: The Anchor
description: Waterfront pub on Silver Street with a large terrace. Perfect for a pint by the river.

name: The Alexandra Arms
description: CAMRA Good Beer Guide regular. Real ale focus and a friendly local vibe.

name: Elm Tree
description: Tiny backstreet pub with an impressive rotating selection of real ales and ciders.

name: Baron of Beef
description: Traditional pub near the river with a good range of ales and pub food.

name: The Cambridge Blue
description: Multi-room pub with a huge beer garden. Extensive ale and cider list.

name: The Free Press
description: Small, no-nonsense pub with a loyal following. No music, no fruit machines.

name: The Granta
description: Riverside pub with a terrace overlooking the mill pond. Popular for summer drinks.

name: The Haymakers
description: Community pub in Chesterton with real ales and a welcoming atmosphere.

name: The Mitre
description: Historic pub on Bridge Street with wood-panelled interior and a solid ale selection.

name: The Panton Arms
description: Often frequented by scientists from the Engineering and Chemistry Department of the University of Cambridge.

name: The Pint Shop
description: Craft beer and grill house in a central location. Wide selection of keg and cask.

name: The Royal Standard
description: Unpretentious local on Mill Road with a mixed crowd and regular events.

name: The Blue Moon
description: Small pub with a focus on real ale and live music. LGBTQ+ friendly.

name: BrewBoard Taproom
description: Taproom for local BrewBoard brewery. Fresh cask and keg in the heart of town.

name: The White Horse
description: CAMRA Community Pub of the Year. Six changing real ales, large beer garden, food and live gigs. Dog-friendly.

name: Waggon & Horses
description: Village pub with up to five ales from Elgoods and regional brewers. Burgers and wings cooked in-house.

name: The Red Lion
description: Traditional pub since 1836. Cambridge CAMRA Pub of the Year. Real ale, food, darts and outside seating.

name: The Boot
description: Award-winning gastro pub opposite the village green. Cosy bar with fireplace, garden and terrace.

name: The King's Head
description: Village local between Histon and Cambridge. Real ales, pub food and a relaxed atmosphere.

name: The George
description: Village pub with traditional and Thai food. Cafe by day, pub by evening. Beer garden and sports TV.

name: The Architect
description: Bleached wood pub and dining room featuring a contemporary feel and large windows.

name: The Grapes
description: Renowned pub with live music, real ales and pool table, plus classic British menu and steak night.

name: The Punter
description: Cosy pub overlooking a secluded garden courtyard, with 1930s-era artwork jumbled about the walls.

name: King Street Run
description: Friendly pub with wallpapered ceiling, a 3D TV showing live sport and a games room with pool tables.

name: Cambridge Tap
description: Friendly pub with wallpapered ceiling, a 3D TV showing live sport and a games room with pool tables.

name: Town and Gown
description: Charming, airy gastropub serving international fare, plus cocktails & a fringe theatre.

name: The Grain and Hop Store
description: Housed in a former factory, this large pub branch offers classic pub fare, plus live sports on TV.

name: The Clarendon Arms
description: Traditional pub with an innovative gastro menu, timeless decor and a compact beer garden.

name: Cambridge Brewhouse
description: Playful eclectic decor pub with micro-brewery, plus home-smoked British tapas and sharing platters.

name: Champion of the Thames
description: Traditional, no-nonsense watering hole on a street corner serving Greene King ales.

name: The Burleigh Arms
description: Modern pub with a beer garden offering snacks & meals, plus cask ales & beers from around the world.

name: The Brook
description: Homey hangout offering pub grub, live music & karaoke nights in a casual setting with a beer garden.

name: Smokeworks
description: Bright, industrial diner with neon lights, for barbecue ribs and chicken, plus beers and shakes.

name: The Duke of Cambridge
description: Everyday pub plates and draft beer provided in a warm, relaxed environment with outdoor seating.

name: The Corner House
description: Pub featuring a beer garden & green space, serving a wide menu of food & drink.

name: The Tivoli
description: Housed in an elegant old cinema, this bustling venue serves-up British pub food and real ales.

name: The Live and Let Live
description: Lively pub with a rustic vibe, with a large choice of real ales and traditional bar food.

name: The Tram Depot
description: A pub steeped in history - originally the stables for Cambridge Street Tramways who operated a horse-drawn tramway service from 1880 to 1914.

name: The Earl of Derby
description: Offering fresh and traditional home cooked fair and pub food, they are famous for their substantial Full English Breakfast

name: The Empress
description: This old-fashioned pub with garden and resident pig offers a large choice of real ales and whiskies.

name: The Red Bull
description: Family-friendly pub-restaurant offering pub grub and pizza, TV with live sport and patio seating.

name: The Regal
description: Still resembling a cinema, this Wetherspoon pub offers cheap beer and regular meal deals.

name: The Six Bells
description: Traditional wood-panelled pub with patio garden offering real ales, food and some live music.

name: The Green Dragon
description: Hung with flower baskets, this old-world inn with a riverside garden serves real ales and pub grub.

name: The Old Ticket Office
address: Unit 1, Cambridge Railway Station, Cambridge CB1 2JW
description: A magnificent pub at Cambridge railway station offering a range of craft beers, ciders, wines, and spirits.

name: The Station Tavern
description: This comfortable restaurant & bar with exposed-brick walls offers a classic English menu & pints.

name: Sir Isaac Newton
description: Convivial boozer offering burgers and pub grub, sports events on large screens, darts and pool.

name: The Prince Regent
description: Traditional tavern serving pub standbys such as burgers and roasts in modern-meets-traditional digs.

name: The Alma
address: The Alma, 26 Russell Ct, Cambridge CB2 1HW
description: Traditional pub with hardwood floors, old-time beer barrels and memorabilia adorning the walls.

name: The Portland Arms
description: Lively pub with real ales and a courtyard garden, plus 200-capacity live music venue.

name: The Dobblers Inn
description: Pizza and bar snacks, quiz nights and 5 large screens in a simple local boozer with a courtyard.

name: The Milton Arms
description: A family-friendly pub restaurant perched on the picturesque outskirts of Cambridge.

name: The Geldart
description: Traditional pub with regular live music, a classic menu and special dishes served on hot rocks.

name: The Kingston Arms
description: Laid-back pub with a sizable garden, serving locally sourced dishes, wine & beers.

name: The Petersfield
description: Low-key, wood-accented pub with a garden offering classic British fare, including Sunday roasts.

name: Caverley's Brewery
description: Founded by two brothers in 2014, who are intent on brewing a diverse range of beer inspired by the worlds eclectic beer styles.

name: The Devonshire Arms
description: Community pub with a wood burner, real ales, cider & a Caribbean fusion menu.
